title: viola tricolor subvar. hymettia
intro: Viola tricolor subvar. hymettia, also known as the Spring Pansy, is a subspecies of the Common Pansy, characterized by its distinctive yellow and purple markings on its petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Viola tricolor subvar. hymettia have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, and is also dried and used in capsules or as a powder.
